title: Generate a data-dictionary workbook with per-variable frequency tables
summary: Writes an Excel workbook listing every variable with its name, label, storage type, SPSS measure, missing-value codes, and — for categorical variables — a frequency table of observed codes with labels, counts, and percentages.
description: A variable is treated as categorical when its distinct-value count is less than or equal to `cutoff` (default 100). Categorical variables get a frequency table rendered on their per-variable sheet or inline on the dictionary sheet (depending on workbook size); continuous / free-text variables get descriptive summaries (min/max/mean) instead. SPSS value labels and missing-code ranges are read via readstat; CSV/Excel inputs have no metadata and use detected types only.

arguments.name: output_dir
arguments.description: Directory for the output .xlsx; created if missing.

arguments.name: cutoff
arguments.description: Max distinct values before a variable is treated as continuous (frequency table suppressed).

notes: The resulting workbook is the canonical metadata carrier for this plugin: `write-sav` can read it back via `data_dictionary_path` to hydrate labels and missing codes on a CSV-based build.
`cutoff` is a pragmatic ceiling, not a statistical rule — raise it for surveys with long enumeration lists, lower it to force more variables to be treated as continuous.
